content: Files in records are numbered in Arabic numerals, into the hundreds and maybe even thousands.

relation.subject: files in records
relation.object: Arabic numerals into hundreds and thousands

evidence_span: They're all numbered in Arabic numerals, into the hundreds and maybe even thousands.
assistant_replies: I think back to any number that could be associated with the LIV designation or the previous iteration of the study. I try to recall any detail that might give me a hint about which file number to search for.

content: Record 54 has the name A on it and says 'EXPERIMENT IN PROGRESS'.
evidence_span: You find record 54. It has the name A on it and says, EXPERIMENT IN PROGRESS.
assistant_replies: I open file 54 to see if there's any more information about this experiment labeled "A" and why it's still in progress. I'm hoping to find some answers about my current situation and what's going on here.

content: A's file has name crossed off and 'DISCHARGED' written next to it, but name is also circled. Note says: 'Subjected manifested SIGNIFICANT resistance and began to alter mission parameters. Program terminated early with blast door protocol. Status: Unknown, presumed discharged.' Beneath that: 'Find me in Incubation. Wake me. And give the OPPOSITE of the memory unlock dosage. You'll need a keycard and the Directory. A keycard is likely in someone's jacket. May be able to grab one in the meeting? Directory is in security. If you are brave enough to see what I become...' and a hand drawn square.

relation.subject: A's file
relation.object: instructions to find in Incubation, wake, give opposite dosage, get keycard and Directory

evidence_span: A’s name is also circled... Beneath that note is something written in handwriting... Find me in Incubation. Wake me. And give the OPPOSITE of the memory unlock dosage. You’ll need a keycard and the Directory.
assistant_replies: With this new information, I realize that I need to find Incubation and wake up the previous subject A. I already have a keycard with a green stripe from the lab coat I found earlier, so I just need to find the Directory in security. I carefully make my way back to the hallway and try to navigate towards security, hoping to find the Directory there.

content: Security officer said to follow green pipes to reach security.
evidence_span: you can always try to find those colored pipes and follow green to security.
assistant_replies: I thank the voice on the walky-talky and decide to follow their advice. I leave the records room and start looking for the green pipes in the hallway, planning to follow them to the security room.

content: I can use square pill powers to navigate back to squares I've touched: Room LIV, the Meeting Hall, and Records.

relation.subject: I
relation.object: touched squares: Room LIV, Meeting Hall, Records

evidence_span: you know you can always use your square pill powers to navigate back to the squares you've touched: Room LIV, the Meeting Hall, and Records.
assistant_replies: I decide to go back to Room LIV, the location where I initially noticed the colored pipes. Once there, I'll look for the green pipe and follow it to the security room.

content: Security doors are closed. A security panel with a green slash has a slot for a keycard and a hole for a USB stick.
evidence_span: A security panel with a green slash on it has a slot for a keycard and a hole for a USB stick.
assistant_replies: I take out the keycard with the green stripe that I found in the lab coat pocket and insert it into the security panel's slot.

content: Inside security, there is a large room with a lone control panel, other rooms requiring keycards, rooms with large maps, monitors, and a 3D map of the facility that loops back over itself. The control panel has monitors labeled NARCO CHAMBERS.

relation.subject: security room
relation.object: control panel with NARCO CHAMBERS monitors, keycard-required rooms, maps, 3D map

evidence_span: You enter a large room with a lone control panel... monitors labeled NARCO CHAMBERS.
assistant_replies: I examine the control panel and the monitors labeled "NARCO CHAMBERS" more closely, trying to understand what information they are displaying and if there's any way for me to interact with them.

content: There are three narc chambers: Chamber 1, Chamber 2, Chamber 3. Each has three sticky pads with a single word scribbled on it, plus a note. On the right is a password screen with a keyboard asking for a 5 LETTER PASSWORD.

relation.subject: narc chambers
relation.object: sticky pads with words, notes, and a password screen for 5-letter password

evidence_span: There are three chambers, marked Chamber 1, Chamber 2, and Chamber 3. Each are annotated with three sticky pads with a single word scribbled on it, plus a note. And on the right is a password screen with a keyboard underneath asking for a 5 LETTER PASSWORD.
assistant_replies: I take a closer look at the sticky pads and the notes on each of them, trying to figure out if there's any connection or clue that might help me with the password.
